title: The week's top stories, April 14-17 (MarketWatch)
author: MarketWatch staff
abstract: MarketWatch 'week's top stories' roundup (Apr 17 2003, 6:06 p.m. EST) covering April 14-17, 2003 — the week that included Intel's Q1 2003 earnings (reported Tuesday Apr 15 2003) and Apple's earnings. Peter Kastner, chief research officer at Aberdeen Group, provides the Intel commentary: 'Given the global economic uncertainty, Intel has executed with some dexterity and deserves a lot of credit for their numbers.' Intel stock rose ~$2 to $18.66 by week-end. Article frame is the dot-com-bust-plus-Iraq-war-economic-uncertainty backdrop. Apple posted a 65% YoY earnings decline and denied rumors of a Universal Music bid. Other stories covered: Bush $550B tax cut, weekly market wrap-up. Kastner's Intel quote captures the 'Intel-as-defensive-holding' framing characteristic of spring-2003 analyst commentary.
importance_rationale: Brief Kastner Intel cameo inside a MarketWatch weekly roundup. Not a standalone analytical piece; the Kastner quote is a short credit-for-Q1-numbers framing. Valuable primarily as a datable record of Kastner's April 2003 Intel view during the post-bust / pre-recovery window.
relevance_rationale: Spring-2003 market-wrap with Iraq-war-economic-uncertainty framing is a historical artifact. Kastner's 'Intel executed with dexterity' observation is about Q1 2003 specifically.
prescience_rationale: Kastner's comment is a retrospective assessment of Intel's just-reported Q1 2003 results, not a forward prediction.
